svg.text: TTLSim 22V10 burns — GAL22V10 test harness on Arduino Mega 2560
The combinational burn uses ALL 12 inputs, so this harness needs the full outer row plus five inner-row pins. All directions fixed; no series resistors.
GAL22V10 / ATF22V10 — test socket (24-pin, 0.3" ZIF recommended)

g.text: C1: 100 nF ceramic across pins 24–12, close to the socket

svg.text: All pin directions are fixed across both burns — the Mega only ever drives GAL inputs and only ever reads OLMC pins. No series resistors needed.
Physical view — bottom-edge header, left → right (outer row fully used + five inner pins)
board edge below • every outer-row pin is used; inner-row pins D52 / D50 (inputs i, j) and D30 / D28 / D26 (outputs 16 / 15 / 14) join them
Per-burn pin assignments (pin 24 = 5 V, pin 12 = GND)

g.text: y21 = a # d, oe = e & k ²
² Hi-Z proof for y21: INPUT_PULLUP on D29; with e&k = 0 and a = d = 0 (driven y21 would be 0), reading 1 proves the pin floated. The 22V10's tri-state is a per-OLMC .oe product term.

³ Pin 17 is an unused OLMC in both burns — left unconnected. OLMC pins unused by only one burn (20/22 combinational; 15/16/18/19 registered) are wired, but only ever as Mega INPUTS, so safe either way.

⁴ The 22V10 has NO global /OE — pin 13 is a plain array input on this part. AR (rst) clears all registers asynchronously, level-sensitive; SP (pre) presets all registers to 1 synchronously, at the clock edge only.
Clocking (registered burn): pulse D53 low→high→low per step; read while low. Preset also sets qb0's register, so pin 20 (active-low !qb0) reads 0 after SP and 1 after AR.
